content:
•

Getting married! 🥳

I had fun creating a table plan that's easy to query for both main use cases, "What's my table?" then "Who else is at my table?". With relations between people and tables represtented as strings. (picture)

•

Congratulations!! I love that way of thinking about a seating plan.

Also: looks like the link is broken

•

I’d like to continue my development streak of >150 days on Hister. My plan for this week is to add content import features for
several bookmark managers.

My initial targets are:

• Karakeep

• Linkwarden

• Linkding

I’d appreciate recommendations for other services to support.

•

I moved from Karakeep to Readeck, perhaps there's something for you to dig into? :)

•

Readeck is definitely another good candidate, thanks!

BTW, why did you switch?

•

Wow! Thats so exciting! Loving that every feature that I wish you added, you’re working on. Adding Hoarder will be amazing.

Potential suggestions: paperless-ngx, RSS feeds (with the option to delete items once read from feed reader,

•

That's awesome. But I do wonder what's the likelihood of you getting banned or heavily rate limited by attempting to crawl a bunch of websites you visit often? Especially now that AI companies are crawling en masse and scummy companies are selling unconsenting user traffic for those that want to bypass firewalls.

I'm guessing trying to achieve the sweetspot of how much you can do before that happens is a very fine line? I'd be interested to hear your thoughts on that.

•

Recovering from a nasty fall off my bicycle last week... I believe I may have cracked one of my ribs, because my chest still hurts badly.

On topic-wise, I'm tweaking my website. I realized I'm not too fond of its current design and if I'm to shake it up, I might as well try to do it in a way that passes most of the nuHTML and/or Dillo checks.

•

Work: need to build some new images, fix a script that broke, continue overseeing training of new vendor. Gotta write some Terraform and work on some observability dashboards, too.

IRL: Went to the doctor last week and learned that my blood pressure is a little high, also saw that my weight reached a number that I always said would be my signal that I need to get on top of my physical health, so I'm joining a gym. Going to set an exercise plan and try to maintain it for a few weeks to see how it goes and then try to dive into preparing for a 70.3 triathlon.

•

Lately I've been working on my own implementation of the Nand2Tetris suite that is used to follow the course and the book. I used it a few years back with the java version and then the web version and both suck in different ways. So I'm taking the matter into my own hands and writing a C++20 alternative that uses Dear ImGUI.

My goal is to provide better error messages, have good performance and be very easy to use.
I have implemented the assembler, disassembler, hack emulator and am working through getting all the other tools in there slowly.

https://github.com/RaphGL/N2T_Suite

•

Trying out Kimi K3 vs Claude Opus 4.8 (Fable is too token-hungry for me). It's pretty good. I got it to build a new feature for Pagecord and gave Claude the same prompt, and the features were almost identical. This was quite unexpected to be honest, but almost everything down to the method names, model names, etc were the same! It's certainly slower than Claude, and did make a couple of sloppy mistakes... but still, for $39/mo vs $90 I'll probably switch for a month. Well, as soon as it's not "sold out" which it is right now!

•

I have done some research on agentic workflows and caching that I will be publishing hopefully tomorrow. To help people building agentic harnesses save some money and time. I'll try to submit a PR to pi.dev as part of the core or maybe as an extension (will need to talk to that team about what makes the most sense).

Outside of that I have more updates to Thetix to deploy (https://thetix.ai). Better quality for about the same price. I also ship updates pretty much everyday to Abject (https://abject.world) which is a research project that I ended up using daily.

•

I recently restarted an OMAD diet/routine after a long break. I'm hoping to do a longer fast this week - probably 72 hours to start with, and then we'll see how long I can go on. Having been into intermittent fasting for a decade, I've never done longer fasts - hoping to change that now.

•

[Comment removed by author]

•

Apparently I’m going to be doing some research into vestibular migraines and heartburn and potential comorbidities. Had my first migraine in a decade last night, followed by waking up at 3am with brutal heartburn that’s only starting to fade now, four hours later. Haven’t figured out any potential triggers yet, yesterday was a very ordinary Sunday.

•

This week I’m updating WordJoy, a Chinese-character puzzle game, based on player feedback.

The current demo is a compact solitaire-style puzzle: component cards are dealt into seven stacks, and only the exposed top cards can be moved. A component can stack only onto one with exactly one more stroke, so you need to untangle the layout and uncover the highlighted target components. Release them in the required order to complete two Chinese characters and clear the level.

The demo currently includes six levels. I’d especially appreciate feedback on rule clarity and whether the character-building goal works for players who don’t read Chinese.
Demo: https://chunq.itch.io/wordjoy

•

•

Getting everything done before I can enable my OoO and spend two weeks swimming in Austrian mountain lakes.

•

I just finished a recent blog post on AI usage, so I'm back to working on my conference talk on Ada. I'm focusing on correctness techniques you can try out easily and borrow for other languages or tools.

•

Had to travel to the office in Tennessee this week. Will be attending my company's leadership cohort. I need to decide if I am going to talk to my boss' boss about how or lack of planning and focus over the last 6 years is costing us millions in VMWare mistakes or just wait for my next job offer to hit my inbox and take it.

•

I'm taking a look at CMYK colors to author directly in a custom game engine
